single_responsibility.name: Single Responsibility Principle
single_responsibility.description: A class should have only one reason to change. Each module, class, or function should encapsulate a single part of the functionality.
indicators: class has >7 public methods
class imports >8 modules
function has >5 parameters
function has >3 distinct responsibilities
class mixes I/O, business logic, and data transformation
single_responsibility.recommendation: Split into focused classes/functions, each handling one concern. Extract helpers for distinct responsibilities and use composition to combine them.

open_closed.name: Open/Closed Principle
open_closed.description: Software entities should be open for extension but closed for modification. You should be able to add new behavior without changing existing code.
indicators: excessive if/elif chains for type dispatch
switch statements or conditionals on object type
modifying existing classes to add new behavior instead of extending
adding new feature requires changes in >3 existing files
open_closed.recommendation: Use polymorphism, strategy pattern, or plugin architectures. Define abstract base classes and add new behavior by creating new subclasses rather than modifying existing ones.

liskov_substitution.name: Liskov Substitution Principle
liskov_substitution.description: Objects of a superclass should be replaceable with objects of a subclass without breaking the program. Subtypes must honor the contracts of their base types.
indicators: subclass overrides method and changes behavior semantics
isinstance checks in code that receives a base type
NotImplementedError raised in subclass for inherited methods
subclass strengthens preconditions or weakens postconditions
liskov_substitution.recommendation: Ensure subclasses fulfill the full contract of the base class. If a subclass cannot support an inherited method, reconsider the inheritance hierarchy and prefer composition over inheritance.

interface_segregation.name: Interface Segregation Principle
interface_segregation.description: No client should be forced to depend on methods it does not use. Prefer many small, specific interfaces over one large, general-purpose interface.
indicators: classes with many unused or no-op method implementations
interfaces or abstract base classes with >7 methods
clients that import a class but only use 1-2 of its methods
implementing classes that raise NotImplementedError for several inherited methods
interface_segregation.recommendation: Break large interfaces into smaller, role-specific ones. Clients should depend only on the methods they actually use. Use protocols or ABCs with minimal method sets.

dependency_inversion.name: Dependency Inversion Principle
dependency_inversion.description: High-level modules should not depend on low-level modules; both should depend on abstractions. Abstractions should not depend on details.
indicators: direct instantiation of dependencies inside methods
importing concrete classes instead of abstract interfaces
no dependency injection or inversion of control
high-level business logic directly calling database or file system APIs
dependency_inversion.recommendation: Depend on abstractions (protocols, ABCs) rather than concrete implementations. Use dependency injection to provide concrete instances at runtime. Define interfaces at the boundary of each layer.

long_method.description: Method is too long to understand at a glance
long_method.threshold: Function exceeds 50 lines or cyclomatic complexity > 10

large_class.description: Class tries to do too much
large_class.threshold: Class has >10 public methods or >300 lines

feature_envy.description: Method uses data from another class more than its own
feature_envy.threshold: Method references another object's attributes >3 times
feature_envy.recommendation: Move the method to the class whose data it uses

data_clumps.description: Same group of variables passed together to multiple functions
data_clumps.threshold: 3+ parameters appear together in >2 function signatures
data_clumps.recommendation: Group into a data class or named tuple

primitive_obsession.description: Using primitive types instead of small objects for simple tasks
primitive_obsession.threshold: Multiple related primitives passed together or validated repeatedly
primitive_obsession.recommendation: Create value objects or data classes
